{{small|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘企画展示時に撮影。}} == 関連施設 == * [[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]（和歌山市岩橋） - 天王塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 * [[和歌山市立博物館]]（和歌山市湊本町） - 天王塚古墳の石室レプリカを展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** 発掘調査報告書 *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=岩橋千塚|publisher=和歌山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|岩橋千塚|1967}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳 -特別史跡岩橋千塚古墳群追加指定に伴う発掘調査報告書-|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|author=|year=2020|chapter=|title=特別史跡 岩橋千塚古墳群 -天王塚古墳 2次・3次発掘調査報告書-|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139273|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|天王塚古墳 2次・3次発掘調査報告書|2020}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** 現地説明会資料 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/haihu.pdf 天王塚山古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料]}}（和歌山県庁生涯学習局文化遺産課、2015年）|ref={{Harvid|発掘調査現地説明会資料|2015}}}} - リンクは和歌山県ホームページ。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kiifudoki.wakayama-c.ed.jp/gennsetu/gennsetusiryou.pdf 天王塚古墳第2次発掘調査現地説明会資料]}}（和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘、2018年）|ref={{Harvid|第2次発掘調査現地説明会資料|2018}}}} - リンクは紀伊風土記の丘ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=和佐古墳群|title=和歌山県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]31|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|和佐古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丹野拓・米田文孝|year=2018|chapter=|title=紀国造家の実像をさぐる 岩橋千塚古墳群|series=シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」126|publisher=新泉社|isbn=9784787718365|ref=}} == 外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2070|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ 天王塚古墳] - 和歌山市教育委員会「和歌山市の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:てんのうつかこふん わかやまし}} [[Category:岩橋千塚古墳群]] [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text: [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}
== External Links ==
* {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education]

[[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.
== References ==

== Related Articles ==

[[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[ja:天王塚古墳]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)-qq]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

[[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[ja:天王塚古墳]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)-qq]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

'''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

'''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)-qq]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}
== External Links ==
* {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education]

'''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>

revision.text:
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)-qq]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}
== External Links ==
* {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)-qq]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] <!--Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)-->[[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳)
The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}.
It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

It is the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture<ref group="note" name="size">Major kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height">The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.<ref name="Wakayama City">[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education.</ref>
The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan.<ref group="note" name="chamber_height"/> Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads.
Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period.
Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.<ref name="Special Historic Site">{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}</ref>
== Burial Chamber ==
The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor
Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present.
Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017.<ref name="Wakayama City"/>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber.

'''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long.</ref> and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]]

Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - 石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |出土品 = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |出土品 = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]]

Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka
