[[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |出土品 = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue ware, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * （岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The scale reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * 高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |ISO = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位の規模
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designated {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. Artifacts include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. Artifacts found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits artifacts from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * 石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a designation1 {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber dimensions are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a other_designation1 {{ill|Special other_other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a other_designation1 {{ill|Special other_other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == Burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * {{National other_other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a other_designation1 {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == * 墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 24.37 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} (Wasa area), a other_designation1 {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special other_designation of Japan|ja|特別史跡}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National other_designation Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} == External Links == *
