{{small|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘企画展示時に撮影。}} == 関連施設 == * [[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]（和歌山市岩橋） - 天王塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 * [[和歌山市立博物館]]（和歌山市湊本町） - 天王塚古墳の石室レプリカを展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** 発掘調査報告書 *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=岩橋千塚|publisher=和歌山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|岩橋千塚|1967}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳 -特別史跡岩橋千塚古墳群追加指定に伴う発掘調査報告書-|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大谷山22号墳、天王塚古墳|2016}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|author=|year=2020|chapter=|title=特別史跡 岩橋千塚古墳群 -天王塚古墳 2次・3次発掘調査報告書-|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139273|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|天王塚古墳 2次・3次発掘調査報告書|2020}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** 現地説明会資料 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/haihu.pdf 天王塚山古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料]}}（和歌山県庁生涯学習局文化遺産課、2015年）|ref={{Harvid|発掘調査現地説明会資料|2015}}}} - リンクは和歌山県ホームページ。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kiifudoki.wakayama-c.ed.jp/gennsetu/gennsetusiryou.pdf 天王塚古墳第2次発掘調査現地説明会資料]}}（和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘、2018年）|ref={{Harvid|第2次発掘調査現地説明会資料|2018}}}} - リンクは紀伊風土記の丘ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=和佐古墳群|title=和歌山県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]31|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|和佐古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丹野拓・米田文孝|year=2018|chapter=|title=紀国造家の実像をさぐる 岩橋千塚古墳群|series=シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」126|publisher=新泉社|isbn=9784787718365|ref=}} == 外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2070|岩橋千塚古墳群}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ 天王塚古墳] - 和歌山市教育委員会「和歌山市の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:てんのうつかこふん わかやまし}} [[Category:岩橋千塚古墳群]] [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]]

高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |alternate_name = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 24.37 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 14|coordinates = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]

Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/schist|qq=}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama City Museum|qq=}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |alternate_name = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 24.37 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 14|coordinates = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama, Wakayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}. It has a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|qq=}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|qq=}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in {{ill|Ōnokutsu Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnokutsu Kofun|qq=}} (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with {{ill|schist|ja|結晶片岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/schist|qq=}}, the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * {{ill|Wakayama City Museum|ja|和歌山市立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama City Museum|qq=}} - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|岩橋千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]

It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|qq=}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|qq=}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |alternate_name = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 24.37 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 14|coordinates = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|qq=}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|qq=}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]]

（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |alternate_name = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 24.37 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 14|coordinates = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|qq=}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|qq=}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]]

It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|qq=}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|qq=}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:岩橋千塚古墳群]]

（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|qq=}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|qq=}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[Category:和歌山市の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:岩橋千塚古墳群]]

The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:岩橋千塚古墳群]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天王塚古墳 |image = [[File:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |alternate_name = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 24.37 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 14|coordinates = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|qq=}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|qq=}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[en:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase Senzuka Kofun Group]]

墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |alternate_name = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 24.37 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 14|coordinates = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|qq=}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|qq=}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|qq=}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|qq=}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|qq=}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]][[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]]}} [[sn:Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[sn:ennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase Senzuka Kofun Group]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}}
