It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |alternate_name = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[:en:和歌山県|和歌山県]][[:en:和歌山市|和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 24.37 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 14|coordinates = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}}

石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}}. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}}

高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

墳丘（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |alternate_name = 天王塚山古墳 |所属 = [[岩橋千塚古墳群]]（和佐地区） |location = [[:en:和歌山県|和歌山県]][[:en:和歌山市|和歌山市]]吉礼・下和佐 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 24.37 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 14|coordinates = 16.41 |omitted = JP-30 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長88m
高さ10.1m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 両袖式{{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
（岩橋型、石棚・石梁付） |burial goods = 玉類・鉄製品・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]中葉 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[特別史跡]]「岩橋千塚古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = 和歌山県第1位のarea
石室高さは全国第2位 |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} The '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' (天王塚古墳), also known as '''Tennōzuka-yama Kofun''' (天王塚山古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] structure and is part of the [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] (Wasa area), a Historic Site1 {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}}. It is the largest kofun in Wakayama PrefectureMajor kofun in Wakayama Prefecture include: 1. '''Tennōzuka Kofun''' - 88m long (Wasa area). 2. {{ill|Kurakake no Koshikofun|ja|車駕之古址古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurakake no Koshikofun|1=Kurakake no Koshikofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637339}} - 86m long. 2. {{ill|Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|ja|大日山35号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnichi-yama 35th Mound|1=Dainichiyama Tomb No. 35|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529436}} - 86m long (Iwase-Senzuka cluster). 4. {{ill|Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|井辺八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibe Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Inbe Hachiman-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373771}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|ja|大谷山22号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani-yama 22nd Mound|1=Ōtaniyama Tomb No. 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55452706}} - 70m long. 4. {{ill|Ōtani Kofun|ja|大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Kofun|1=Ōtani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97312140}} - 70m long. and was constructed around the mid-6th century during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It features one of the tallest {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in Japan.The tallest stone chamber in Japan is found in [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōnokutsu Kofun]] (Kumamoto), at 6.48m high. == Overview == Located atop Tennōzuka-yama (approx. 155m elevation) within the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster, Tennōzuka Kofun holds the distinction of being the largest mound and chamber in the cluster. Excavations were conducted in 1964 and from 2015 onwards.[http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun], Wakayama City Board of Education. The kofun is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward, with a two-tiered mound. It measures 88m in length, making it the largest in Wakayama Prefecture. No [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], or external structures have been found. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening southward, characteristic of the Iwase style, with stone shelves and beams. The chamber height is an impressive 5.9m, Ranking second in Japan. burial goods include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], beads, glass ornaments, horse gear, and iron arrowheads. Estimated construction dates back to the mid-6th century. The map_size reflects the prominence of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} during that period. Designated as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} in 2016 as part of the Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster.{{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} == burial Chamber == The stone chamber area are as follows: * Total Length: 10m * Main Chamber: 4.22m long, 2.89m wide, 5.9m high * Entrance Passage * Corridor Constructed with [[:en:Schist|schist]], the chamber features stone shelves and eight stone beams. Drainage channels run from the chamber floor through the corridor, and scattered floor stones are present. burial goods found include beads, iron items, and Sue pottery, discovered during excavations in 1964 and 2017. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum]] (Wakayama City) - Exhibits burial goods from Tennōzuka Kofun. * [[:en:Wakayama City Museum|Wakayama City Museum]] - Displays a replica of the stone chamber. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|2070|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/6512/ Tennōzuka Kofun - Wakayama City Board of Education] {{translated page|ja|天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)|version=104551973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天王塚古墳 (和歌山市)]] [[ja:天王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Tennozuka Kofun (Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q48763062}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Wakayama]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
